x-enumeration-literals.name: Automatic Rollover Execution
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The rollover is executed automatically by the system at maturity without requiring customer action.

Comment
Example:
A term deposit automatically renews for the same term at the prevailing rate.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Conditional Rollover Execution
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The rollover is executed automatically only if predefined conditions are satisfied.

Comment:
Example:
Loan automatically rolled over only if repayment history is clean.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Manual Rollover Execution
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The rollover is executed only when initiated by a bank officer or operations staff.

Comment:
Example:
Corporate loan rollover processed manually after credit committee approval.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Scheduled Rollover Execution
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The rollover is pre-scheduled to execute on a specific date/time event.

Comment:
Example:
Deposit renewal scheduled at midnight on maturity date.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Customer-Initiated Execution Mode
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The rollover occurs only when the customer actively requests or confirms it.

Comment:
Example:
Customer renews a fixed deposit through online banking before maturity.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Approval-Based Execution Mode
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The rollover requires internal or external approval before execution.

Comment
Example:
Syndicated loan rollover requires majority lender approval.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Hybrid Execution Mode
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Combination of two or more execution modes.

Comment:
Example:
Automatic but subject to condition check
Customer-initiated but requires approval
